scope: Gated /admin area — magic-link login + repo management dashboard (is_featured, is_hidden, manual refresh).

scope_decision.summary: Login UI always shows 'Se o e-mail estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado.' regardless of allowlist status. Only mateushr23@gmail.com actually receives a magic link; all other addresses get nothing. The callback route (handled in Stage 4b) is the secondary gate — it verifies the email against the allowlist server-side and redirects non-allowlisted sessions to /admin/login?error=denied.
scope_decision.why: Reveals no signal about which emails are in the allowlist — blocks account-enumeration for a single-user admin. Matches Supabase's own recommended pattern for magic-link + allowlist. Users in the allowlist get zero friction; attackers get zero information. Deny-on-callback alone would confirm to an attacker that their guessed email exists the moment they see the 'denied' page after paste — worse UX signal leakage.

tokens_added.name: --color-success
tokens_added.justification: Desaturated sage green — signals 'link enviado' confirmation and is-featured toggle ON state. Stays within warm-neutral family (green with warm bias, not cool emerald). Distinct from amber accent so a user can read toggle-on and link-sent states without confusion. Contrast on primary bg: 4.6:1 (AA body).

tokens_added.name: --color-danger
tokens_added.justification: Warm terracotta — used ONLY for inline form error copy ('e-mail inválido', rate-limit message) and the callback denial banner. Desaturated (~48% sat). Never used as a button fill — only as border-l-2 on error messages. Contrast on primary: 4.7:1.

tokens_added.name: --color-surface-admin
tokens_added.justification: Half-step between --color-primary and --color-surface. Used ONLY as the admin table row hover band — lets the row feel interactive without introducing card shadows that would violate the no-card editorial rule.

components_to_build.name: AdminHeader
components_to_build.layout_description: Mirrors SiteHeader height (h-16) and container (max-w-[1100px], px-5 md:px-12). Left: wordmark 'mateushr' + amber dot + mono caption 'admin' in text-muted. Right: email in mono text-sm (truncated on mobile) and a 'sair' text link that POSTs to /admin/logout. No primary nav — admin is single-page. Bottom hairline border-b border-border is always visible (not scroll-toggled) to signal authenticated mode vs. public.

components_to_build.name: MagicLinkForm
components_to_build.props: { action: (formData: FormData) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }> }
components_to_build.layout_description: Single-column form, max-w-[420px], mx-auto. Vertically: eyebrow 'ADMIN · ACESSO' in mono uppercase; Fraunces italic display-sized heading 'entrar'; body_lg paragraph '1 linha explaining magic-link flow'; label 'e-mail' above input; email input (native type=email, required, autocomplete=email, inputmode=email); submit button full-width. Below button: danger-bordered error slot (empty by default, border-l-2 border-danger + pl-3 when populated). Uses useFormStatus + useActionState (React 19) for pending/error state — button label switches to 'enviando…' with disabled + aria-busy. No external loading spinner.

components_to_build.name: MagicLinkConfirmation
components_to_build.layout_description: Replaces MagicLinkForm after successful submit (rendered by /admin/login when ?sent=1 search param is present). Same max-w-[420px] column. Eyebrow 'ADMIN · ACESSO'; Fraunces italic 'link enviado'; body paragraph using success-tinted em dash prefix: 'Se {submittedEmail} estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado. Confira sua caixa de entrada — o link expira em 10 minutos.'; below: a single mono text link 'enviar novo link' that links back to /admin/login (clears the ?sent param). Intentionally does NOT say 'check your email' without the conditional 'se estiver autorizado' — preserves the always-send UX.

components_to_build.name: ToggleSwitch
components_to_build.props: { name: string; defaultChecked: boolean; label: string; labelHidden?: boolean; onToggle: (next: boolean) => Promise<void>; disabled?: boolean }
components_to_build.layout_description: Hand-rolled from native checkbox. See toggle_switch_spec below for full visual and behavioral spec.

components_to_build.name: RefreshButton
components_to_build.props: { action: () => Promise<{ ok: boolean; count?: number; error?: string }> }
components_to_build.layout_description: Mono text-sm button with amber left border-l-2 (never a filled box — matches editorial non-card rule). Label 'atualizar agora' with a small rotating arrow glyph '↻' that spins (animation-name: spin; animation-duration: 900ms; animation-iteration-count: infinite; linear) only while aria-busy=true. On success: label swaps to 'atualizado · {count} repos' in success color for 3s then resets. On error: swaps to 'falhou — tentar novamente' in danger color. aria-live='polite' region adjacent announces the result for screen readers.

components_to_build.name: AdminRepoRow
components_to_build.props: { repo: Repo; toggleFeatured: ServerAction; toggleHidden: ServerAction }
components_to_build.layout_description: Extends the editorial index row aesthetic but adapts for admin controls. Desktop grid: [48px number | 1fr name+meta | 120px featured-toggle | 120px hidden-toggle | 80px github-arrow]. Mobile: collapses to 2 rows — row 1 name+meta, row 2 two toggles inline with labels visible. No full-row <a> (unlike IndexRow) because toggles need their own click targets — instead the repo name itself is a focused anchor to repo.url (target=_blank) and the rest of the row is non-interactive container. Border-b border-border. Hover state changes bg to --color-surface-admin. Each toggle passes the repo.id via a bound Server Action so updates round-trip via React 19 form actions — no client fetch code.

components_to_build.name: AdminRepoList
components_to_build.props: { repos: Repo[]; toggleFeatured: ServerAction; toggleHidden: ServerAction }
components_to_build.layout_description: Section with eyebrow 'REPOS · {repos.length} TOTAL · {hiddenCount} OCULTOS'. Below: header row (also grid-styled, text-dim mono eyebrow labels 'NUM / NOME / DESTAQUE / OCULTO / GITHUB'); then <ol role='list'> of AdminRepoRow. If repos.length === 0, renders EmptyAdminState instead of the list. Sort order: is_hidden ASC then pushed_at DESC (shows visible repos first so admin reviews active state before hidden archive).

components_to_build.name: EmptyAdminState
components_to_build.layout_description: Centered in the admin main region. Eyebrow 'ÍNDICE VAZIO' in mono; short Fraunces italic 'nenhum repo indexado'; body copy 'Clique atualizar agora para buscar repos públicos do GitHub.'; then embedded RefreshButton. No illustration — stays editorial.

components_to_build.name: AuthDeniedBanner
components_to_build.layout_description: Rendered inside /admin/login ONLY when search param error=denied is present (callback hit but email not in allowlist). Sits above MagicLinkForm. Danger-tinted: border-l-2 border-danger + pl-4, mono text-sm, single line 'Este e-mail não tem permissão de acesso.' role='alert'. Does NOT reveal what email was attempted — echoes nothing back.

routes_to_build.rendering_strategy: RSC page with a Server Action for the magic-link request (action is bound to MagicLinkForm). Conditional branches: default state = MagicLinkForm; ?sent=1&email=<b64> = MagicLinkConfirmation; ?error=denied = AuthDeniedBanner above MagicLinkForm.
routes_to_build.auth_guard: No guard — public route. Middleware in proxy.ts should short-circuit /admin/login when the user is ALREADY authenticated (redirect to /admin) to avoid a confusing re-login prompt.
routes_to_build.layout_description: min-h-[calc(100dvh-64px)] flex vertically centered. Container max-w-[420px], px-5. Shares root layout (pt-BR, fonts, PaperGrain). Page has <meta name='robots' content='noindex,nofollow'/> via generateMetadata. No site nav — only minimal AdminHeader with no email shown (user not signed in).

routes_to_build.rendering_strategy: RSC page. Reads session from Supabase server client, reads ALL repos (including is_hidden=true) via server-side Supabase with the service role key (NEVER shipped to client). Renders AdminHeader + AdminRepoList. Data mutations happen via two Server Actions (toggleFeatured, toggleHidden) defined in src/app/admin/actions.ts and one Server Action for manual refresh (refreshRepos).
routes_to_build.auth_guard: Two-layer guard. (1) proxy.ts blocks /admin* for unauthenticated requests, redirecting to /admin/login. (2) The page itself re-verifies the session and that the session email === allowlist entry; if mismatch, it calls redirect('/admin/login?error=denied') — defense in depth against middleware bypass.
routes_to_build.layout_description: Same root layout. <meta name='robots' content='noindex,nofollow'/>. Main region uses the same max-w-[1100px] container. Suspense boundary wraps AdminRepoList with a <AdminListSkeleton/> fallback (10 mono-labeled skeleton rows — see empty_states.loading).

routes_to_build.rendering_strategy: Route Handler — POST only. Calls Supabase signOut(), clears cookies, redirects to /admin/login. GET returns 405.
routes_to_build.auth_guard: n/a — destructive intent on any session state.

toggle_switch_spec.pattern: Native <input type='checkbox'> visually hidden (sr-only), wrapped by a <label> that is the visual track. The label uses a ::before pseudo-element as the thumb. This is fully accessible (checkbox semantics, space-to-toggle, form submission works) with zero external deps.

dimensions.hit_area: min 44x44px achieved via label padding py-2 px-1 — the visual track is 40x22 but the clickable label extends to 44+ in both directions

visual_states.off: track bg: --color-surface-elevated; track border: 1px solid --color-border; thumb: --color-text-dim; thumb translateX(0)
visual_states.on: track bg: rgba(107, 143, 113, 0.2) — success-tinted; track border: 1px solid --color-success; thumb: --color-success; thumb translateX(18px)
visual_states.on_featured_variant: For is_featured ONLY, track bg uses amber: rgba(232, 164, 76, 0.2); thumb: --color-accent. Semantic signal: amber = promoted/featured (matches home accent); sage = simple ON (hidden toggle)
visual_states.focus_visible: track gets outline: 2px solid --color-accent; outline-offset: 3px (inherits global :focus-visible rule on the hidden checkbox — proxied to track via :has(:focus-visible))
visual_states.hover: track border color shifts to --color-border-strong; no thumb movement
visual_states.disabled: opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; no hover state
visual_states.loading: When parent sets data-pending=true (during Server Action await), thumb shows a 900ms infinite pulse via opacity 1 -> 0.5 -> 1; track border flashes amber at 0.3 opacity. aria-busy=true on the label

toggle_switch_spec.animation: transition: transform 220ms var(--ease-out-expo), background-color 220ms var(--ease-out-expo), border-color 220ms var(--ease-out-expo). Disabled entirely under prefers-reduced-motion (falls back to instant state change).
toggle_switch_spec.keyboard: Space toggles (native checkbox behavior). Tab moves focus in/out. Enter does NOT toggle (native checkbox rule — submit happens only if inside a <form>, which it is; submission is handled by the Server Action bound to the form).
toggle_switch_spec.aria: Native checkbox provides role and checked state automatically. Label wraps input for implicit association. An adjacent visually-hidden <span role='status' aria-live='polite'> announces 'Destaque ativado' / 'Oculto desativado' after each server round-trip resolves (announced from the Server Action's success toast, passed as prop into a tiny client helper).

empty_states.login_link_sent: See MagicLinkConfirmation. Does not confirm the email was in the allowlist — copy always says 'Se {email} estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado.' Uses mono for email to make the echo feel like system output, not marketing.
empty_states.admin_zero_repos: See EmptyAdminState — mono eyebrow 'ÍNDICE VAZIO', Fraunces italic 'nenhum repo indexado', body instructs clicking refresh. No illustration.
empty_states.admin_loading: <AdminListSkeleton/> renders 10 rows matching AdminRepoRow grid. Each skeleton row shows: (col 1) mono '--' placeholder; (col 2) a 1px bottom border only — no shimmer, no pulse, just empty space with correct heights. Consistent with 'no card soup' rule — loading state should not introduce skeuomorphic gray blocks where the real UI has no filled boxes. Suspense fallback height matches expected 10-row list to prevent CLS.
empty_states.admin_auth_denied: See AuthDeniedBanner. Rendered above MagicLinkForm on /admin/login?error=denied. Generic single-line message. Does NOT echo the attempted email.
empty_states.admin_refresh_zero_new: After RefreshButton success with count=0, label becomes 'atualizado · 0 novos' in text-muted (not success green) for 3s. Neutral tone — not an error, not a win.

microcopy_inline.login_intro: Digite seu e-mail. Se estiver autorizado, enviaremos um link de acesso válido por 10 minutos.
microcopy_inline.login_error_generic: Não foi possível enviar. Tente novamente.
microcopy_inline.confirmation_body: Se {email} estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado. Confira sua caixa de entrada.
microcopy_inline.auth_denied_message: Este e-mail não tem permissão de acesso.
microcopy_inline.empty_admin_body: Clique atualizar agora para buscar repos públicos do GitHub.
voice_rules: Mono is used for system output (emails, counts, timestamps); Geist is used for human-read labels and body; Fraunces italic only for page-level headings ('entrar', 'link enviado', 'nenhum repo indexado')

a11y_notes.login_page_tab_order: skip-link -> admin wordmark (link to /) -> email input -> submit button -> (if visible) resend/back link. No stray tab stops. AuthDeniedBanner is role=alert so it is announced once on mount without stealing focus.
a11y_notes.admin_page_tab_order: skip-link -> admin wordmark -> logout link -> RefreshButton -> (for each row) repo-name anchor -> featured-toggle -> hidden-toggle -> github-arrow anchor. 5 tab stops per row × repos — acceptable for an admin of a single-user vault.
a11y_notes.focus_management_after_submit: After MagicLinkForm submits successfully, Server Action returns { redirect: '/admin/login?sent=1&email=<b64>' } which triggers a full page navigation. On the new page, focus is set to the 'enviar novo link' anchor via a small 'use client' helper that calls .focus() on mount (autoFocus does not apply to anchors). This prevents focus from being lost to <body>.
a11y_notes.focus_management_after_denied: When landing on /admin/login?error=denied, the AuthDeniedBanner has tabindex=-1 and is .focus()'d on mount so screen readers announce it immediately.
a11y_notes.aria_live_toggle_changes: Each ToggleSwitch is followed by a sibling <span role='status' aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' class='sr-only'> whose textContent updates to the new state label after the Server Action resolves. One live region per toggle — not a global one — so announcements don't pile up when multiple toggles are flipped quickly.
a11y_notes.aria_live_refresh: RefreshButton has an adjacent aria-live='polite' region announcing 'Buscando repositórios…' on click and 'Atualizado: {count} repos' on success; 'Falha ao atualizar' on error.
a11y_notes.focus_not_obscured_wcag_22: scroll-margin-top: 80px global rule already applied in globals.css covers admin anchors. Admin header is sticky with the same h-16 height — no additional work needed.
a11y_notes.keyboard_only_testing: Verify the full flow with only keyboard: Tab through login, submit, land on confirmation, back to login, mock-auth, land on /admin, toggle each switch, click refresh. No mouse.
a11y_notes.reduced_motion: Existing global prefers-reduced-motion block in globals.css covers new transitions (toggle thumb slide, refresh spinner spin, skeleton). Toggle state changes become instant; refresh spinner does not spin (label still changes).
a11y_notes.contrast_verification: --color-success #6B8F71 on #0B0B0C = 4.6:1 (AA pass for body). --color-danger #C96E5A on #0B0B0C = 4.7:1 (AA pass). --color-accent on #101012 (row hover bg) = 7.1:1. All new tokens pass WCAG AA.
a11y_notes.touch_targets_wcag_22: ToggleSwitch label uses py-2 px-1 to reach 44px tall clickable area despite the visual 22px track. RefreshButton has py-3 px-4. All toggles and buttons meet WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 minimum (24px) and the recommended 44px.

security_notes.allowlist_single_source_of_truth: The allowlist ['mateushr23@gmail.com'] MUST live server-side only — either hardcoded in a server-only module (src/lib/admin/allowlist.ts with import 'server-only') or in SUPABASE_ADMIN_ALLOWLIST env var parsed as comma-separated. NEVER in NEXT_PUBLIC_* envs. NEVER shipped in a client bundle.
security_notes.no_email_enumeration: Login UI NEVER confirms whether an email is in the allowlist. Confirmation copy always reads 'Se {email} estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado.' Submit always takes the same time (±50ms) regardless of allowlist match — the Server Action calls Supabase signInWithOtp only when the email matches, but pads the response with a fixed 300ms setTimeout on non-match to equalize timing.
security_notes.no_service_role_in_client: Service role key (bypasses RLS, needed to read is_hidden rows) used ONLY in RSC / Server Actions / Route Handlers. The client bundle uses the anon key. Verify by grepping the built bundle for the service-role key prefix.
security_notes.csrf_on_server_actions: Next.js Server Actions have built-in CSRF via origin checks in recent versions — verify next.config.ts has experimental.serverActions.allowedOrigins set to the production domain only.
security_notes.cookie_flags: Supabase auth cookies must be HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax. Verify via Set-Cookie response header after login.
security_notes.rate_limit_login: Stage 4b should add a rate limit on the magic-link Server Action: max 5 requests per IP per 15 minutes, max 3 per email per hour. UI reveals a generic 'Muitas tentativas. Espere 1 minuto.' error (login_error_rate_limit copy hook).
security_notes.noindex_admin: All admin routes include <meta name='robots' content='noindex,nofollow'/> via generateMetadata. proxy.ts additionally returns X-Robots-Tag: noindex header for defense in depth.
security_notes.admin_not_in_public_nav: SiteHeader (public) has zero link to /admin. The route is reachable only by direct URL entry. Sitemap.xml must exclude /admin*.
security_notes.toggle_authorization: Every Server Action (toggleFeatured, toggleHidden, refreshRepos) MUST re-verify session.user.email is in the allowlist as its first line. Do not trust the middleware alone. Log any mismatch as a security event.
